title: ferray-core: flat_index uses .iter().nth() — O(n) for non-contiguous arrays
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

flat_index() in basic.rs iterates through elements with self.inner.iter().nth(idx), which is O(n) for non-contiguous (strided) arrays. NumPy's flat indexing computes the physical offset directly from shape and strides in O(ndim) time using unravel_index. The current implementation is correct but degrades to O(n) for large non-contiguous arrays (e.g., transposed or sliced). Fix: compute the multi-dimensional index from the flat index (unravel), then use stride arithmetic to get the offset in O(ndim).
